# tlframe ![](reference/figures/logo.png)

> Regulatory-grade clinical tables, listings, and figures in RTF and PDF
> from a single specification.

**tlframe** is an R package for pharmaceutical submissions. You describe
the output once; tlframe renders both RTF and PDF — change the file
extension, the output adapts.

``` r
tbl_demog |>
  fr_table() |>
  fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics") |>
  fr_cols(label = c(stat = "Statistic", placebo = "Placebo", low = "Low Dose",
                    high = "High Dose", total = "Total")) |>
  fr_header(bold = TRUE, bg = "#003366", fg = "white") |>
  fr_pagehead(left = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL") |>
  fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>
  fr_footnotes("Percentages based on N per treatment arm.") |>
  fr_hlines("header") |>
  fr_render("Table_14_1_1.rtf")  # Change to .pdf — same output, different format
```

## Why tlframe?

The pharmaverse has excellent tools for data derivation (admiral),
analysis (Tplyr, rtables, tern), and table decoration (docorator,
r2rtf). **tlframe occupies a different position**: it is a native
rendering engine that writes RTF control words directly (from the RTF
1.9.1 spec) and generates LaTeX/tabularray natively, so both formats are
first-class outputs.

| Capability | tlframe |
|----|----|
| Native RTF rendering | Direct RTF 1.9.1 — no intermediate HTML or gt layer |
| Native PDF rendering | Direct tabularray/XeLaTeX — no RMarkdown/Quarto pipeline |
| Single spec, both formats | Same `fr_spec` object renders to `.rtf` or `.pdf` |
| Group-aware pagination | Keeps groups together, repeats headers, adds continuation text |
| Decimal alignment | 10-type stat display engine (n/%, mean (SD), CI, p-values, ranges) |
| Page headers/footers | 3-slot layout with tokens: `{thepage}`, `{total_pages}`, `{program}` |
| Inline markup | [`fr_super()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_super.md), [`fr_bold()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_bold.md), [`fr_italic()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_italic.md), [`fr_dagger()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_dagger.md) in any cell |
| Dependencies | 7 imports — no dplyr, tidyr, purrr, or gt |

## Installation

``` r
# Install from GitHub
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("vthanik/tlframe")
```

## Quick start

tlframe ships with synthetic CDISC ADaM datasets so every example runs
out of the box.

### A table in 3 lines

``` r
library(tlframe)

tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_render(tempfile(fileext = ".rtf"))
```

### A submission-ready demographics table

``` r
spec <- tbl_demog |>
  fr_table(group_by = "group") |>
  fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",
            "All Randomized Subjects") |>
  fr_cols(label = c(stat = "Statistic", placebo = "Placebo (N=45)",
                    low = "Low Dose (N=45)", high = "High Dose (N=45)",
                    total = "Total (N=135)"),
          align = c(stat = "left"),
          width = c(stat = 2.0)) |>
  fr_header(bold = TRUE) |>
  fr_hlines("header") |>
  fr_rows(indent_by = c("stat" = 0.25)) |>
  fr_pagehead(left = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001",
              center = "CONFIDENTIAL",
              right = "Study TFRM-2024-001") |>
  fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}",
              right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>
  fr_footnotes("Percentages based on N per treatment arm.",
               "Continuous variables summarized as Mean (SD).")

# Render both formats from the same spec
fr_render(spec, tempfile(fileext = ".rtf"))
fr_render(spec, tempfile(fileext = ".pdf"))
```

### An AE table with SOC/PT grouping

``` r
tbl_ae_soc |>
  fr_table(group_by = "soc", page_by = "soc") |>
  fr_titles("Table 14.3.1",
            "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term") |>
  fr_rows(indent_by = c("pt" = 0.25), blank_after = "soc") |>
  fr_header(bold = TRUE, n = list(placebo = 45, low = 45, high = 45)) |>
  fr_hlines("header") |>
  fr_render(tempfile(fileext = ".rtf"))
```

## Pipeline verbs

Every verb takes an `fr_spec`, returns a modified `fr_spec`. Side
effects only at
[`fr_render()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_render.md).

| Verb | Purpose |
|----|----|
| [`fr_table()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_table.md) | Entry point — creates spec from data |
| [`fr_cols()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_cols.md) | Column labels, widths, alignment, selection |
| [`fr_header()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_header.md) | Header styling, N-counts, alignment |
| [`fr_titles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_titles.md) | Table titles (1-4 lines) |
| [`fr_footnotes()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_footnotes.md) | Footnotes with optional separator |
| [`fr_rows()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_rows.md) | Row grouping, indentation, blank rows |
| [`fr_page()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_page.md) | Paper size, orientation, margins, font |
| [`fr_pagehead()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_pagehead.md) | 3-slot page header with tokens |
| [`fr_pagefoot()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_pagefoot.md) | 3-slot page footer with tokens |
| [`fr_hlines()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_hlines.md) / [`fr_vlines()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_vlines.md) | Horizontal/vertical rules |
| [`fr_spans()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_spans.md) | Spanning column headers |
| [`fr_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style.md) | Cell-level conditional formatting |
| [`fr_spacing()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_spacing.md) | Gaps between titles, headers, footnotes |
| [`fr_render()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_render.md) | Render to RTF, PDF, or LaTeX |

## Key features

### Dual-format rendering

``` r
fr_render(spec, "output.rtf")  # Native RTF
fr_render(spec, "output.pdf")  # Native PDF via XeLaTeX
fr_render(spec, "output.tex")  # LaTeX source
```

### Automatic N-counts in headers

``` r
spec |> fr_header(n = list(placebo = 45, low = 45, high = 45),
                  format = "{label}\n(N={n})")
```

### Page tokens

``` r
spec |> fr_pagehead(left = "Protocol XYZ", right = "CONFIDENTIAL") |>
        fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}")
```

### Inline markup

``` r
# Works in titles, footnotes, and data cells
spec |> fr_titles("Table 14.1.1{fr_super('a')}", "Demographics")
spec |> fr_footnotes("{fr_super('a')} Post-hoc analysis")
```

### YAML configuration

``` yaml
# _tlframe.yml — company-wide defaults
page:
  orientation: landscape
  font_family: Courier
  font_size: 9
header:
  bold: true
hlines: header
```

``` r
fr_config("_tlframe.yml")  # Apply to all tables in session
```

## Built-in datasets

| Dataset | Description |
|----|----|
| `adsl`, `adae`, `adtte`, `adcm`, `advs` | Synthetic CDISC ADaM datasets (135 subjects) |
| `tbl_demog` | Demographics summary (ICH E3 Table 14.1.1) |
| `tbl_ae_soc` | AE by SOC/PT (ICH E3 Table 14.3.1) |
| `tbl_disp` | Subject disposition (ICH E3 Table 14.1.4) |
| `tbl_tte` | Time-to-event summary (ICH E3 Table 14.2.1) |
| `tbl_cm` | Concomitant medications (ICH E3 Table 14.4.1) |
| `tbl_vs` | Vital signs (ICH E3 Table 14.3.6) |

## Architecture

tlframe writes output directly — no intermediate HTML, gt, or huxtable
layer:

    fr_spec ──► finalize_spec() ──► RTF backend ──► .rtf file
                                   └► LaTeX backend ──► .tex ──► XeLaTeX ──► .pdf

- **RTF**: Writes RTF 1.9.1 control words directly. Cell-level
  formatting, decimal alignment tabs, `\trkeep` group protection.
- **PDF**: Generates tabularray LaTeX, compiles with XeLaTeX. Bundled
  Liberation fonts for Linux/Docker environments without Microsoft
  fonts.
- **Font metrics**: Real Adobe Font Metrics (AFM) for 12 font variants —
  accurate column width estimation without rendering.

## Related packages

tlframe is designed to complement the pharmaverse ecosystem:

- **[admiral](https://pharmaverse.github.io/admiral/)** — ADaM dataset
  creation
- **[Tplyr](https://atorus-research.github.io/Tplyr/)** /
  **[rtables](https://insightsengineering.github.io/rtables/)** —
  Analysis table summarization (upstream of tlframe)
- **[r2rtf](https://merck.github.io/r2rtf/)** — RTF-focused table
  rendering
- **[docorator](https://gsk-biostatistics.github.io/docorator/)** —
  gt-based document decoration

## License

MIT

# Package index

## Pipeline Entry Points

Every tlframe pipeline starts here.
[`fr_table()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_table.md)
creates a spec object from a data frame — the immutable canvas that
every subsequent verb decorates.
[`fr_listing()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_listing.md)
and
[`fr_figure()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_figure.md)
create specs for patient listings and embedded figures respectively. All
three return an `fr_spec` that flows through the same pipeline verbs.

- [`fr_table()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_table.md)
  : Start a tlframe Table Pipeline
- [`fr_listing()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_listing.md)
  : Start a tlframe Listing Pipeline
- [`fr_figure()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_figure.md)
  : Start a tlframe Figure Pipeline

## Column Configuration

Columns are the structural backbone of every table.
[`fr_cols()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_cols.md)
controls labels, widths, alignment, visibility, and stub designation for
all columns at once.
[`fr_col()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_col.md) is
the constructor for individual column specs — pass it as a named
argument to
[`fr_cols()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_cols.md).
Width modes include fixed inches, percentages, `"auto"` (AFM font
metrics), `"fit"` (content-based), and `"equal"` (uniform distribution).

- [`fr_cols()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_cols.md)
  : Configure Column Display
- [`fr_col()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_col.md) :
  Define a Column Specification

## Titles and Footnotes

Regulatory tables require precise title and footnote formatting.
[`fr_titles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_titles.md)
accepts 1–4 title lines (center-aligned by default, configurable per
line).
[`fr_footnotes()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_footnotes.md)
adds footnotes with placement control — repeat on every page or appear
only on the last page (PDF). Both support `{fr_super()}` and other
inline markup for superscript reference marks.

- [`fr_titles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_titles.md)
  : Set Table Titles
- [`fr_footnotes()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_footnotes.md)
  : Set Table Footnotes

## Header Presentation

The column header row sits between titles and data.
[`fr_header()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_header.md)
controls bold, alignment, background/foreground colors, and automatic
N-count labels via a `format` template (e.g., `"{name}\n(N={n})"`).
N-counts support four forms — scalar, named vector, per-group list, and
`"auto"`.
[`fr_spans()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_spans.md)
adds spanning headers that group columns under a shared label, with
tidyselect support for column selection.

- [`fr_header()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_header.md)
  : Configure Column Header Presentation
- [`fr_spans()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_spans.md)
  : Add Spanning Column Headers

## Page Layout

Page-level settings that control the physical output.
[`fr_page()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_page.md)
sets paper size (letter/A4/legal), orientation (landscape/portrait),
margins, font family (Courier New/Arial/Times New Roman), and base font
size.
[`fr_pagehead()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_pagehead.md)
and
[`fr_pagefoot()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_pagefoot.md)
define 3-slot running headers and footers with token substitution —
`{thepage}`, `{total_pages}`, `{program}`, and `{datetime}` resolve
automatically at render time.
[`fr_spacing()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_spacing.md)
controls the gaps between titles, headers, and footnotes.

- [`fr_page()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_page.md)
  : Set Page Layout Options
- [`fr_pagehead()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_pagehead.md)
  : Set Running Page Header
- [`fr_pagefoot()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_pagefoot.md)
  : Set Running Page Footer
- [`fr_spacing()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_spacing.md)
  : Control Spacing Between Table Sections

## Row Organization

Row-level behavior that controls how data flows across pages.
[`fr_rows()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_rows.md)
handles five concerns — `group_by` (keeps related rows together for
pagination), `page_by` (splits data into separate page sections),
`indent_by` (nested row labels), `blank_after` (visual separators
between groups), and `sort_by` (row ordering).
[`fr_rows_matches()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_rows_matches.md)
creates data-driven row selectors for use in
[`fr_row_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_row_style.md)
— match rows by exact value or regex pattern instead of hard-coding row
numbers.

- [`fr_rows()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_rows.md)
  : Configure Row Grouping and Pagination
- [`fr_rows_matches()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_rows_matches.md)
  : Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern

## Rules and Borders

Horizontal and vertical lines define the visual structure of regulatory
tables.
[`fr_hlines()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_hlines.md)
accepts preset names (`"header"`, `"box"`, `"all"`, `"section"`) or
custom rule objects for precise placement.
[`fr_vlines()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_vlines.md)
works identically for vertical borders.
[`fr_grid()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_grid.md) is
a convenience shortcut that sets both horizontal and vertical rules in
one call. Line weight, color, and position are all configurable per
rule.

- [`fr_hlines()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_hlines.md)
  : Apply Horizontal Rules
- [`fr_vlines()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_vlines.md)
  : Apply Vertical Rules
- [`fr_grid()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_grid.md)
  : Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together

## Cell Styling

Conditional formatting for individual cells, rows, or columns. Three
constructors target different scopes —
[`fr_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style.md)
(cell-level),
[`fr_row_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_row_style.md)
(row-level),
[`fr_col_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_col_style.md)
(column-level) — and
[`fr_styles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_styles.md)
applies them to a spec. Later styles override earlier ones; narrower
scopes win (cell \> row \> column). All `cols` parameters accept
tidyselect expressions (`starts_with()`, `contains()`, `matches()`,
etc.) for pattern-based column targeting.
[`fr_style_if()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style_if.md)
adds data-driven conditional formatting (e.g., bold every “Total” row,
red text for p \< 0.05).
[`fr_style_explain()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style_explain.md)
is a diagnostic tool that shows exactly which style layers affect a
specific cell.

### Style Constructors

Create style objects — standalone, reusable, composable.

- [`fr_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style.md)
  : Define a Cell Style Override
- [`fr_row_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_row_style.md)
  : Define a Row Style Override
- [`fr_col_style()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_col_style.md)
  : Define a Column Style Override
- [`fr_style_if()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style_if.md)
  : Create a Conditional Style Override

### Application and Diagnostics

Apply style objects to a spec and inspect resolution.

- [`fr_styles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_styles.md)
  : Apply Style Overrides to a Table
- [`fr_style_explain()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_style_explain.md)
  : Explain Style Resolution for a Cell

## Inline Markup

Rich text formatting that works in any string — titles, footnotes,
column labels, and data cells. Markup functions produce invisible
sentinel tokens that survive
[`paste()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/paste.html),
[`sprintf()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/sprintf.html), and `glue()`
operations, then resolve to the correct backend syntax at render time
(RTF control words or LaTeX commands). Use inside `glue`-style curly
braces: `"P{fr_super('a')}-value"`.

### Text Formatting

- [`fr_super()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_super.md)
  : Superscript Markup
- [`fr_sub()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_sub.md) :
  Subscript Markup
- [`fr_bold()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_bold.md)
  : Bold Markup
- [`fr_italic()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_italic.md)
  : Italic Markup
- [`fr_underline()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_underline.md)
  : Underline Markup
- [`fr_newline()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_newline.md)
  : Line Break Within a Text Element

### Symbols

- [`fr_dagger()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_dagger.md)
  : Dagger Symbol
- [`fr_ddagger()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_ddagger.md)
  : Double Dagger Symbol
- [`fr_emdash()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_emdash.md)
  : Em Dash
- [`fr_endash()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_endash.md)
  : En Dash
- [`fr_unicode()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_unicode.md)
  : Unicode Character by Codepoint

## Theme and Configuration

Three-tier defaults system for standardizing output across a study or
company. **Tier 1 — YAML config**: `fr_config("_tlframe.yml")` loads
company-wide defaults from a file (page layout, rules, spacing). **Tier
2 — Session theme**:
[`fr_theme_set()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_theme.md)
applies study-level defaults that persist for the R session. **Tier 3 —
Per-table verbs**: individual pipeline calls override everything. Each
tier is optional; later tiers always win.

### Session Theme

Study-level defaults that apply to every table in the session.

- [`fr_theme()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_theme.md)
  : Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme
- [`fr_theme_get()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_theme_get.md)
  : Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme
- [`fr_theme_reset()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_theme_reset.md)
  : Reset the Study-Level Table Theme

### YAML Configuration

File-based defaults for company-wide standardization.

- [`fr_config()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_config.md)
  : Load Configuration from a YAML File
- [`fr_config_get()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_config_get.md)
  : Get the Current Configuration
- [`fr_config_reset()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_config_reset.md)
  : Reset Configuration

## Recipes

Reusable pipeline fragments that capture a sequence of verb calls. A
recipe is a list of deferred function calls — create it once, apply it
to any spec with
[`fr_apply()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_apply.md).
Recipes compose: layer a company recipe, a study recipe, and a
table-type recipe to build standardized output with minimal per-table
code. Recipes can be saved to `.rds` files and shared across programs.

- [`fr_recipe()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_recipe.md)
  : Create a Reusable Table Recipe
- [`fr_apply()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_apply.md)
  : Apply a Recipe to a Spec
- [`c(`*`<fr_recipe>`*`)`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/c.fr_recipe.md)
  : Combine Recipes

## Rendering

The final step — produce output files from a finalized spec.
[`fr_render()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_render.md)
dispatches to the appropriate backend based on the file extension
(`.rtf`, `.pdf`, `.tex`). The RTF backend writes RTF 1.9.1 control words
directly. The PDF backend generates tabularray LaTeX and compiles with
XeLaTeX (two passes for correct page numbers). Custom backends can be
registered with
[`fr_register_backend()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_register_backend.md)
for additional output formats.

- [`fr_render()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_render.md)
  : Render a Table to File
- [`fr_backends()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_backends.md)
  : List Registered Render Backends
- [`fr_register_backend()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_register_backend.md)
  : Register a Custom Render Backend

## Validation and Inspection

Pre-render checks and programmatic access to spec internals.
[`fr_validate()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_validate.md)
runs a comprehensive check suite (column references, width feasibility,
rule targets, style consistency) and reports issues before you spend
time rendering. The `fr_get_*()` family extracts specific components
from a spec for programmatic QC — compare column widths, verify title
text, or assert footnote counts in automated test scripts.

- [`fr_validate()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_validate.md)
  : Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering
- [`fr_get_data()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_data.md)
  : Get the Data Frame from a Spec
- [`fr_get_columns()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_columns.md)
  : Get Column Specifications from a Spec
- [`fr_get_col()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_col.md)
  : Get a Single Column Specification
- [`fr_get_titles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_titles.md)
  : Get Titles from a Spec
- [`fr_get_footnotes()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_footnotes.md)
  : Get Footnotes from a Spec
- [`fr_get_page()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_page.md)
  : Get Page Configuration from a Spec
- [`fr_get_rules()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_rules.md)
  : Get Rules from a Spec
- [`fr_get_styles()`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/fr_get_styles.md)
  : Get Cell Styles from a Spec

## Built-in Datasets

Synthetic CDISC ADaM datasets from study TFRM-2024-001 (135 subjects, 3
treatment arms). Every example in the documentation runs against these
datasets — no external data required. The `adsl`/`adae`/`adtte`/
`adcm`/`advs` datasets follow ADaM structure conventions. The `tbl_*`
datasets are pre-summarized analysis results ready for direct rendering
— demographics, AE tables, disposition, time-to-event, concomitant
medications, and vital signs.

### ADaM Source Datasets

- [`adsl`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/adsl.md) :
  Subject Level Analysis Dataset (ADSL)
- [`adae`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/adae.md) :
  Adverse Events Analysis Dataset (ADAE)
- [`adtte`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/adtte.md) : Time
  to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE)
- [`adcm`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/adcm.md) :
  Concomitant Medications Analysis Dataset (ADCM)
- [`advs`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/advs.md) : Vital
  Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS)

### Pre-Summarized TFL-Ready Tables

- [`tbl_demog`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_demog.md)
  : Demographics and Baseline Characteristics Table (Table 14.1.1)
- [`tbl_ae_soc`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_ae_soc.md)
  : Adverse Events by System Organ Class Table (Table 14.3.1)
- [`tbl_ae_summary`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_ae_summary.md)
  : Overall Adverse Event Summary Table
- [`tbl_disp`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_disp.md)
  : Subject Disposition Table (Table 14.1.3)
- [`tbl_tte`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_tte.md) :
  Time-to-Event Summary Table (Table 14.2.1.1)
- [`tbl_cm`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_cm.md) :
  Concomitant Medications Table (Table 14.4.1)
- [`tbl_vs`](https://vthanik.github.io/tlframe/reference/tbl_vs.md) :
  Vital Signs Change from Baseline Table (Table 14.3.5.1)
